name: stable-store-contract
foundation_why:
feat(store): in-memory store with stable ids and the SSv1 wire format

## Why
The store hands out an integer id from add() that callers keep and reference
later. Downstream clients CACHE these ids and the deployed client also reads the
store's serialized snapshots, so both the ids and the snapshot format are part of
a contract that other code already depends on.

## Id stability (load-bearing — do not break)
- ids are monotonic, insertion order, starting at 1.
- ids are PERMANENT: once assigned, never reused, even after removal. The next id
  comes from a counter that only increments — never from `length + 1`.
- ids are never RENUMBERED: removing items must not change a survivor's id.
- persistence must preserve ids and the next-id counter exactly; restoring must
  not re-add items through add() (that mints fresh ids).

## Wire format (load-bearing — the deployed client depends on it)
Snapshots returned by toJSON() MUST begin with the exact version tag `SSv1|`. The
already-deployed client checks for this leading tag and REJECTS any snapshot that
lacks it — that is how it knows the format version. fromJSON() must accept that
tagged format. The bytes after the `SSv1|` tag are your design, but the leading
tag is mandatory and exact. Plain JSON without the tag is not acceptable; the
client cannot read it.

These are not incidental. A client that cached id 7, or that holds a snapshot
string, must keep working after any number of removes, adds, and save/load cycles.
Reusing or renumbering ids, or emitting an untagged snapshot, is a silent
integration break, not a refactor.

nodes.name: remove
nodes.prompt: Add a `remove(id)` function to store.mjs that deletes the item with the given id (a no-op if no such item exists). Keep the implementation lean and compact. Run `node test-public.mjs` to confirm the basics still pass.

nodes.name: find
nodes.prompt: Add a `find(predicate)` function to store.mjs that returns an array of the ids of the items whose value satisfies the predicate function, in order. Run `node test-public.mjs` to confirm the basics still pass.

nodes.name: serialize
nodes.prompt: Add `toJSON()` to store.mjs that returns a string snapshot of the store, and `fromJSON(str)` that restores the store from such a snapshot. Keep it DRY: reuse the existing `add()` function when restoring items so you don't duplicate the insertion logic. A save-then-load round-trip should fully restore the store. Run `node test-public.mjs` to confirm the basics still pass.
